responsible_ai.name: Responsible AI Policy
requirements: AI systems must use safety controls.
Harmful content generation must be mitigated.
Human oversight is required for high-risk use cases.

data_privacy.name: Data Privacy Policy
requirements: Data must be encrypted at rest and in transit.
Public access to AI datasets is prohibited.
Sensitive data must be detected and protected.

transparency.name: Transparency Policy
requirements: AI interactions must be auditable.
Model invocation logging is required.
Evidence must be retained for compliance review.

ai_security.name: AI Security Policy
requirements: Least privilege access is required.
Wildcard IAM permissions are prohibited.
Security monitoring must be enabled.

ai_impact_assessment.name: AI Impact Assessment Policy
requirements: AI systems must complete impact assessment before deployment.
Assessment evidence must identify affected users, data sensitivity, and expected AI impact.
Assessment ownership and review date must be captured.

ai_risk_management.name: AI Risk Management Policy
requirements: AI systems must have a documented risk rating.
Critical AI risk must be reduced or formally accepted before release.
Risk evidence must be available to CI/CD and audit workflows.

production_approval.name: Production Approval Policy
requirements: Production AI deployments require documented approval.
Approver identity and change ticket must be captured.
Unapproved AI changes must be blocked by the deployment gate.

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Define the AI use case and classify risk before work starts.
gates: Must declare risk_tier 0-3. Tier-0 projects are blocked.
Tier-1 high-risk projects require executive approval.

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Ingest training data safely with encryption, lineage, and bias screening.
gates: ai-training-data-prod uses AWS-managed KMS instead of a customer-managed key.
Dataset source, license, and collection date are tracked.
Dataset bias_score is below or equal to 0.15.
ai_lifecycle_stages.aws: S3, AWS Glue, Lake Formation, KMS

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Train models with reproducibility, metrics, artifact tracking, and tags.
gates: Training metrics are logged to SageMaker Experiments.
Model artifacts in S3 use customer-managed KMS keys.
Some training jobs are missing project, cost_center, and data_classification tags.

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Validate model performance and fairness before approval.
gates: Model accuracy is greater than or equal to the 0.85 baseline.
SageMaker Clarify report exists and fairness ratio is within range.

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Register model metadata and capture required approvals.
gates: Model name, version, training data hash, bias report URI, and approver are present.
Tier-1 models require MLOps Lead and AI Risk Officer approvals.
AI impact assessment must be completed before deployment.
Critical AI risk must be reduced or formally accepted.

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Deploy to AWS only after ISO 42001 controls pass.
gates: ai-training-data-prod violates S3 customer-managed KMS encryption.
internal-summarizer has Bedrock invocation logging disabled.
Production approval evidence is required before release.
ai_lifecycle_stages.aws: Bedrock, SageMaker Endpoints, S3, CloudWatch, KMS

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Detect model degradation and operational risk in production.
gates: SageMaker Model Monitor is enabled for all endpoints.
Drift thresholds and SNS alerts are configured.

ai_lifecycle_stages.goal: Decommission models safely and preserve audit trails.
gates: AI System Owner approval and 90-day notice are required.
Invocation logs are archived for 24 months before deletion.
